DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Jennet Zapata, Esq. and Catherine Habash, Esq.916-478-7251 Legal Division of Dept. of Fair Emplyment and Housing for Plaintiff Dept. of Fair Employment and Housing Agency.
Brian Ceballo, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CCSF - Dept. of Police Accountability

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views and inquires what rule of law applies addressing the issue on a government agency being a business entity. Defendant argues. Plaintiff Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Department of Fair Employment and Housing on behalf of real party in interest Tanesh Nutall is overruled as to the first cause of action for violation of the Unruh Act and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for violation of Government Code 11135(a). Liberally construed, the complaint sufficiently alleges facts that show that, at the time of the alleged discrimination against Ms. Nutall, the City was conducting itself as a "business establishment" within the meaning of the Unruh Act. Even with a liberal construction, the complaint does not sufficiently allege facts that show that, at the time of the alleged discrimination against Ms. Nutall, the City was engaged in a state-funded "program or activity." Because it is reasonably possible that DFEH can allege such facts, if it can do so in good faith, it is given leave to do so. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for DFEH is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
